title: Nginx Worker Crash — SSL Certificate Expiry
description: Nginx workers crashed after attempting to reload an expired SSL certificate during a config reload. The investigation engine detected the crash pattern in logs, identified the cert expiry as root cause, and auto-executed a service restart after confidence threshold was met.

alert.detail: nginx.service exited with code 1 — 3 worker processes crashed within 60 seconds

hypotheses.cause: Expired SSL certificate causing nginx worker crash on reload
Nginx config syntax error introduced by recent deployment
File descriptor limit reached causing accept() failure

root_cause.summary: The SSL certificate at /etc/nginx/ssl/api.crt expired at 00:00 UTC. A scheduled nginx config reload at 02:00 attempted to load the expired cert, causing all 3 worker processes to exit with SSL_CTX_use_certificate error. No cert renewal automation was configured.
supporting_evidence: openssl verify: certificate has expired (notAfter=Jun  2 00:00:00 2026 GMT)
systemd: nginx.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=1/FAILURE
3 worker process exits within 60s — consistent with reload cascade
No certbot renewal job in crontab — manual cert rotation required
reasoning_steps: CPU 4.1%, memory 28% → system healthy, crash is application-level not resource exhaustion
Logs show SSL_CTX_use_certificate error — cert file exists but rejected by OpenSSL
Timestamp: crashes started at 02:00:01 UTC, matching scheduled reload cron
service_state shows nginx.service failed immediately after reload, not gradual degradation
Root cause confirmed: cert expired 2 hours before crash at 00:00 UTC

outcome.resolution_note: Service restart failed — certificate still expired. Auto-escalated to manual_approval. Operator renewed cert via certbot and restarted. Added cert expiry monitoring alert at 30-day threshold.
